info.title: CeyPay API
info.description:
Accept crypto payments and settle in Sri Lankan Rupees.

The CeyPay API is organised around REST. All requests use HTTPS, accept and return JSON, and are authenticated with an HMAC-SHA256 signature. See [Authentication](/api/v1/authentication) for how to sign a request.

## Environments

Use the base-URL selector on any endpoint to switch the examples between environments.

| Environment | Base URL |
| --- | --- |
| Production | `https://api.ceypay.io` |
| Sandbox | `https://sandbox-api.ceypay.io` |

Which environment serves a request is ultimately determined by **the API key you sign it with**, so a sandbox key stays in the sandbox regardless of the host it is sent to. Sandbox payments are simulated and no real funds move.

servers.description: Sandbox — integration testing, no real funds move

tags.name: Payments
tags.description: Create and track one-off crypto payments. Every payment returns a QR code and a checkout link you can present to the customer.

tags.name: Payment Links
tags.description: Shareable hosted checkout pages. Use them to collect payment over email, SMS, or social media without building a checkout flow.

tags.name: Direct Debit
tags.description: Pre-authorisation contracts that let you charge a customer on demand after they sign once.

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: Recurring billing built on top of a signed Direct Debit contract. CeyPay charges the contract automatically on your billing cycle.

tags.name: Branches
tags.description: Organise payments, links, and contracts across locations, teams, or — if you operate as an aggregator — across your own customers.

tags.name: Withdrawals
tags.description: Request settlement of your unsettled payment balance to your registered bank account.

tags.name: Offramp
tags.description: Convert USDT to LKR and settle directly to a Sri Lankan bank account. Available only to merchants with the `AGGREGATOR` role.

tags.name: Banks
tags.description: Reference data for Sri Lankan banks and their CEFTS codes.

post.description:
Creates a payment order and returns a QR code and checkout link for the customer to pay.

When `currency` is `LKR`, the amount is converted to USDT at the current exchange rate and the response includes the LKR breakdown.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests/minute.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of your payments, newest first.

**Rate limit:** 200 requests/minute.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by payment ID or customer details.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Fetch specific payments by ID. Accepts comma-separated values (`ids=id1,id2`) or repeated params (`ids[]=id1&ids[]=id2`). Maximum 50 IDs.

get.description:
Retrieves a single payment. Only returns payments belonging to your merchant account.

**Rate limit:** 300 requests/minute.

post.description:
Creates a hosted checkout link you can share with customers.

**Rate limit:** 50 requests/minute.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of your payment links.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests/minute.

Retrieves a single payment link.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests/minute.

delete.description:
Deactivates a payment link. This is a soft delete — the link stops accepting new payments but its history is preserved.

**Rate limit:** 50 requests/minute.

get.description:
Returns the scenario codes supported for Direct Debit contracts. A scenario defines the type of service and its transaction limits — pass the returned `id` as `scenarioId` when creating a contract.

**Rate limit:** 200 requests/minute.

post.description:
Creates a pre-authorisation contract for the customer to sign. Returns a QR code and deep link for approval.

For `LKR` contracts, set `slippageBps` to absorb exchange-rate movement between signing and charging. Slippage is rejected on `USDT` contracts.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests/minute.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of Direct Debit contracts for your merchant account.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests/minute.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by merchant contract code or service name.

get.description:
Retrieves a single Direct Debit contract.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests/minute.

post.description:
Queries the payment provider for the contract's latest status and persists it. Use this to check whether the customer has signed.

**Rate limit:** 50 requests/minute.

Terminates a signed contract. Only contracts in `SIGNED` status can be terminated.

**Rate limit:** 50 requests/minute.

terminationNotes.description: Optional note recorded against the termination.

post.description:
Charges a signed contract on demand. No customer interaction is required, so the response contains no QR code or checkout link.

The amount is converted to USDT and must not exceed the contract's `singleUpperLimit` after conversion. You may charge in a different currency than the contract's.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests/minute.

400.description: Contract not signed or expired, amount exceeds the contract limit after conversion, or no exchange rate available.

example.message: Payment 5000 LKR converts to 15.15 USDT, exceeding contract limit 6.06 USDT.

post.description: Creates a recurring billing schedule against a signed Direct Debit contract. CeyPay charges the contract automatically each cycle and retries failed charges according to your retry settings.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of subscriptions for your merchant account.
Retrieves a single subscription.

post.description: Pauses billing. No charges are attempted while a subscription is paused; the schedule resumes from the next cycle after you call resume.

reason.description: Why the subscription is being paused.

post.description: Resumes a paused subscription. Billing continues from the next scheduled cycle.
Cancels a subscription permanently. Cancelled subscriptions cannot be resumed — create a new subscription instead.

reason.description: Why the subscription is being cancelled.

get.description: Returns the billing history for a subscription — one entry per charge attempt, including retries.

post.description:
Creates a branch under your merchant account. Branches created via the API always settle to the merchant's bank account.

**Rate limit:** 60 requests/minute.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of branches for your merchant account.

**Rate limit:** 200 requests/minute.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by branch name or code.

get.description:
Retrieves a single branch.

**Rate limit:** 300 requests/minute.

patch.description:
Updates a branch. Send only the fields you want to change.

**Rate limit:** 60 requests/minute.

delete.description:
Deactivates a branch. This is a soft delete — the branch and its payment history are preserved and remain reportable by `branchId`.

**Rate limit:** 30 requests/minute.

post.description:
Bundles all currently unsettled `PAID` payments into a settlement request and submits it for payout.

Only one active (`PENDING` or `APPROVED`) request is allowed per merchant per day. If the net amount is below the platform's auto-approval threshold the request is approved immediately and the transfer starts; otherwise it stays `PENDING` until a CeyPay admin reviews it.

Requests created via the API record the `apiKeyId` that made them — `requestedBy` will be `null` and `requesterName` will be `"API"`.

**Rate limit:** 10 requests/minute.

branchId.description: Restrict the request to unsettled payments for this branch. Omit to include all merchant-level unsettled payments.

400.description: No unsettled payments, amount below the minimum withdrawal threshold, or a request already exists for today.

403.description: Merchant account is inactive or not approved.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of withdrawal requests together with summary totals for the filtered set.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests/minute.

Retrieves a withdrawal request including the individual payments it settles.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests/minute.

Returns all active Sri Lankan banks and their CEFTS codes. This is static reference data — cache it and reuse the result.

**Rate limit:** 300 requests/minute.

post.description:
Upserts an end-user keyed by your own user ID. Safe to call before every offramp — if the user already exists the existing record is returned.

CeyPay stores only the `externalUserId` you provide. No personal or identifying information about your end-users is collected.

**Rate limit:** 120 requests/minute.

externalUserId.description: The user's ID in your system.

post.description:
Registers a Sri Lankan bank account for an end-user and returns a reusable `userBankId`. An end-user may have several bank accounts, each with its own ID.

**Rate limit:** 60 requests/minute.

get.description:
Returns every bank account registered for an end-user.

**Rate limit:** 200 requests/minute.

Fetches the current USDT/LKR rate and locks it for 60 seconds. Pass the returned `fxLockId` to `POST /v1/aggregator/offramp` before `expiresAt` — expired and already-used locks are rejected.

**Rate limit:** 120 requests/minute.

post.description:
Initiates a USDT → LKR bank transfer against a locked FX rate.

This endpoint is idempotent on `externalRef`. Resubmitting the same key — after a network timeout, for example — returns the original offramp unchanged rather than creating a duplicate transfer.

**Rate limit:** 60 requests/minute.

400.description: Validation error, expired FX quote, or already-used `fxLockId`.

get.description:
Retrieves the current status of an offramp. Poll this, or supply a `webhookUrl` when creating the offramp to be notified instead.

**Rate limit:** 300 requests/minute.

Returns every LKR movement on your float — top-ups, offramp debits, and refunds — with period summary statistics for reconciliation.

**Rate limit:** 60 requests/minute.

Returns a paginated list of offramps with per-status totals and a weighted average FX rate. Each row carries the end-user ID and destination bank details so the export is self-contained.

The `summary` block always covers the full date window regardless of the `status` filter, so per-status breakdowns stay visible even when the item list is filtered.

**Rate limit:** 60 requests/minute.

apiKeyId.name: x-api-key
apiKeyId.description:
Your API key **ID only** — the part before the dot, e.g. `ak_live_abc123`. Never send the secret.

The key also selects the environment: a sandbox key is served by the sandbox environment even on the production host.

timestamp.name: x-timestamp
timestamp.description: Current Unix timestamp in **milliseconds**. Requests older than 5 minutes are rejected.

signature.name: x-signature
signature.description: `HMAC-SHA256(timestamp + method + path + body, SHA256(secret))` as lowercase hex. See [Authentication](/api/v1/authentication).

BadRequest.description: Invalid request body or validation error.

example.message: amount must be a positive number

Unauthorized.description: Invalid API key, signature, or timestamp outside the valid window.

Forbidden.description: The resource belongs to another merchant account.

AggregatorForbidden.description: The resource belongs to another merchant, or your account lacks the `AGGREGATOR` role.

example.message: Aggregator access is not enabled for this account

Conflict.description: The resource conflicts with an existing one — for example a duplicate branch code.

TooManyRequests.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check the `X-RateLimit-Reset` header for when the window resets.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Requests allowed in the current window.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests remaining in the current window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the window resets.

Error.description: Standard error envelope returned by every endpoint.

message.description: Human-readable message, or a list of validation messages.

Currency.description: `USDT` settles directly. `LKR` is converted to USDT at the current rate.

PaymentStatus.description: `INITIATED` — awaiting customer payment. `PAID` — completed. `EXPIRED` — customer did not pay in time. `FAILED` — the payment could not be processed.

PaymentProvider.description: Crypto wallet the customer pays from.

CustomerBillingResponse.description: Billing details echoed back on the payment. Only the fields CeyPay retained are present.

FeeBreakdown.description: Gross-to-net fee breakdown. LKR fields are present only for LKR payments.

branchId.description: Attribute this payment to a branch.

orderExpireTime.description: Expiry as a Unix timestamp in seconds.

webhookUrl.description: URL to receive payment status updates.

qrContent.description: QR payload for wallet scanning. Absent on Direct Debit charges.

checkoutLink.description: Hosted checkout URL. Absent on Direct Debit charges.

directDebitContractId.description: Set when the payment was charged against a Direct Debit contract.

name.description: Name shown on the checkout page.

allowCustomAmount.description: Let the customer enter their own amount.

minAmount.description: Minimum custom amount. Required when `allowCustomAmount` is `true`.

maxAmount.description: Maximum custom amount. Required when `allowCustomAmount` is `true`.

allowQuantityBuy.description: Let the customer buy more than one.

maxQuantity.description: Maximum quantity per purchase. Required when `allowQuantityBuy` is `true`.

reusable.description: Whether the link accepts more than one payment.

slug.description: URL slug for the hosted checkout page.

used_count.description: Number of payments taken through this link.

DirectDebitProvider.description: Direct Debit is currently live on `BINANCE_PAY`.

DirectDebitStatus.description: `INITIATED` — awaiting the customer's signature. `SIGNED` — ready to charge. `TERMINATED` — cancelled. `EXPIRED` — the signing window lapsed.

id.description: Pass this as `scenarioId` when creating a contract.

maxLimit.description: Maximum per-transaction limit for this scenario.

merchantContractCode.description: Your own contract code. Auto-generated when omitted.

serviceName.description: Shown to the customer when they sign.

slippageBps.description: Exchange-rate buffer in basis points for LKR contracts — 10000 bps = 100%. Rejected on USDT contracts.

returnUrl.description: Where to send the customer after they sign.

cancelUrl.description: Where to send the customer if they cancel.

contractId.description: The provider's contract ID, set once signed.

singleUpperLimit.description: Per-charge limit in USDT, including the slippage buffer for LKR contracts.

singleUpperLimitLkr.description: Original LKR limit, on LKR contracts only.

currency.description: May differ from the contract's currency.

amount.description: Amount in `currency`. Must not exceed the contract limit after conversion to USDT.

webhookUrl.description: Overrides the contract's webhook URL for this charge.

SubscriptionStatus.description: `ACTIVE` — billing on schedule. `PAUSED` — billing suspended. `PAST_DUE` — a charge failed and is being retried. `COMPLETED` — reached `maxCycles` or `endDate`. `CANCELLED` — ended early.

contractId.description: A Direct Debit contract in `SIGNED` status.

billingCycleInterval.description: Number of billing cycles between charges — `2` with `MONTHLY` bills every two months.

timezone.description: IANA timezone used to evaluate the billing schedule.

maxCycles.description: Stop after this many successful charges.

spendingCap.description: Stop once total charged reaches this amount.

maxRetryAttempts.description: Retries per failed charge before the subscription goes `PAST_DUE`.

gracePeriodDays.description: Days a `PAST_DUE` subscription stays open before it is cancelled.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs echoed back on the subscription and its webhooks.

code.description: Unique within your merchant account. Map this to your own customer or location ID.

telegramGroupId.description: Telegram group to notify on branch activity.

UpdateBranchRequest.description: All fields are optional — send only what you want to change.

usesMerchantBankAccount.description: Always `true` for branches created via the API.

WithdrawalStatus.description: `PENDING` — awaiting review. `APPROVED` — transfer initiated. `REJECTED` — declined; the payments return to the unsettled pool. `CANCELLED` — withdrawn before review.

bankRefId.description: Reference quoted on the bank transfer.

requestedBy.description: Dashboard user who made the request. `null` for API-created requests.

apiKeyId.description: API key that made the request, when created via the API.

code.description: CEFTS bank code — pass as `bankCode` when registering a bank account.

userId.description: CeyPay's user ID — use this in subsequent requests.

userBankId.description: Pass this as `userBankId` when creating an offramp.

OfframpStatus.description: `PENDING` — queued. `PROCESSING` — transfer in flight. `COMPLETED` — settled, `bankRef` set. `FAILED` — reversed to your float.

fxLockId.description: From `GET /v1/aggregator/quote`. Must not be expired or already used.

externalRef.description: Your idempotency key. Must be unique per transfer — resubmitting returns the original offramp.

currentBalanceLkr.description: Live float balance at request time.

closingBalanceLkr.description: Balance at the last entry on or before `endDate`. `null` without an `endDate`.

type.description: `CREDIT` — float top-up. `DEBIT` — offramp charge. `REFUND` — failed offramp reversal.

OfframpReportSummary.description: Always covers the full date window, regardless of the `status` filter applied to the items.

failedAmountLkr.description: LKR returned to float via refunds.

averageRateUsdtLkr.description: Weighted average rate across `COMPLETED` offramps only.

externalUserId.description: Your internal ID for the end-user.
